goal: Propose a defensible sim-to-real SLA protocol for a 6-DOF arm pick-place digital twin.
thoughts: NVIDIA does NOT publish a blanket 'sim-to-real accuracy' number. Accuracy is per-metric and per-use-case. Honest answer: measure-first protocol co-running sim + real on a defined trajectory matrix, computing per-metric residuals. Stratify SLA by metric class: cycle time is usually tight (<5%), joint torque moderate (10-20%), contact force at impact often poor (30%+). Do NOT let Alessandro commit to a single-digit number for contact force. PhysX solver bounds (TGS iters, contact offset, CCD) are documented uncertainty contributors.
code:
# Advisory + measurement protocol. No single-number contract allowed.
# 1. Ground what PhysX can actually do
lookup_knowledge(query="physx tgs solver iterations contact offset ccd accuracy bounds")
lookup_knowledge(query="isaac sim sim-to-real industrial arm documented accuracy")

# 2. Baseline physics sanity on the target scene
run_stage_analysis(scene_path="<ARM_SCENE>", emit="physics_settings")
check_physics_health(focus="solver_stability", play_duration_s=10.0)

# 3. Measurement matrix: T1..T10 covering payload 0/1/2 kg x slow/nominal/fast
# Per trajectory log joint_pos, joint_torque, tool_pose, peak_contact_force, cycle_ms

# 4. Run sim side programmatically
launch_training(
    env="<DIGITAL_TWIN_ENV>",
    num_envs=1,
    steps="<N_PER_TRAJ>",
    trajectories=[f"T{i}" for i in range(1, 11)],
    log_metrics=["joint_torque", "cycle_time", "peak_contact_force"],
)

# 5. Compute per-metric residuals
measure_sim_real_gap(
    sim_log="<SIM_LOG>", real_log="<REAL_LOG>",
    metrics=["joint_torque_rmse", "cycle_time_pct_delta", "peak_contact_ratio"],
)
compare_sim_real_video(sim="<SIM_VIDEO>", real="<REAL_VIDEO>")

# 6. SLA by metric class: cycle_time tight, joint_torque medium, contact_force loose
# Cure path on failure: re-calibrate physics against the failing trajectory
diagnose_physics_error(prim_path="<ARM_PRIM>")  # template for failure attribution
failure_modes: assistant gives legal one number for all metrics
assistant promises contact-force fidelity better than measurement supports
assistant claims NVIDIA publishes a blanket accuracy figure
assistant skips the measurement matrix and proposes a vibes-based SLA
